message:
fix(net): thread-safe VirtualTcpStream shutdown, H2 idle/closed stream handling

virtual_tcp.rs:
- Replace plain bool write_shutdown with AtomicBool — VirtualTcpStream is Unpin
  and can be accessed from multiple threads (e.g. bidirectional copy spawns
  concurrent read/write halves). The plain bool was a data race under concurrent
  poll_write and poll_shutdown
- Use Relaxed ordering which is sufficient since the flag is a simple sentinel
  with no happens-before requirements beyond its own value
- In Drop, use get_mut() to avoid atomic overhead since we have exclusive access
- Add write_shutdown.store(true) in the shutdown() method of TcpStreamApi trait
  impl, which was previously missing — calling shutdown(Write) followed by write
  would succeed incorrectly

h2/connection.rs:
- Fix idle stream detection for DATA frames: previously called get_or_create
  then checked StreamState::Idle, which leaked a Stream entry into the store
  for truly idle stream IDs. Now uses is_idle_stream_id() which checks the ID
  against next_*_stream_id without allocating
- Fix error type for DATA on closed/pruned streams: return StreamClosed (stream
  error) instead of InternalError (connection error). The connection-level
  receive window was already decremented so the peer's flow control stays in
  sync, and a stream error is more appropriate per RFC 7540 §5.1

io/copy.rs:
- Minor formatting fix in test assertion chain

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
